message:
I've been thinking about home fire safety lately—not in a panic way, just the practical "let's actually have a plan" way. I found this straightforward guide from Ready.gov that breaks down the essentials: escape routes, smoke alarms, all that good stuff you can set up on a regular afternoon. Kind of nice to feel a little more prepared without all the doom.

What's one fire safety thing you've already got in place at home?
